company.name: Ferrite
company.one_liner: Content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs 10x cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet.

sections.snapshot:
## In a nutshell

**Market size is not in the record** (no TAM/SAM/SOM or spend-base cited). The structural problem Ferrite targets is LLM-agent CI regenerating overlapping build work across a fleet, so spend scales with redundant agent steps rather than unique artifacts [C1]. That load is urgent as agent-driven pipelines proliferate: yesterday’s remote cache was built for human-authored, relatively stable graphs; agent runs re-emit steps that a content-addressed cache can collapse [C2]. Ferrite’s product is a content-addressed build cache that deduplicates those agent-generated steps to cut CI cost [C1].

## Company

**Ferrite** — solo technical founder Mara Voss (Berlin, DE); first-time founder, no prior fundraise claimed [C3]. Raising a $500k pre-seed; applying for Example Fund I’s $100k fast check [C4]. Sector fit (ai-infrastructure / devtools) is implied by the product, not independently sized here.

## Traction snapshot (contested vs verified)

Deck claims **3,100 active installations across 240 organizations** and **$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months** [C5][C6]. Public registry shows **2,900 lifetime downloads** and **~340/week** — lifetime downloads sit *below* the claimed active-install count [C7]. That is an open contradiction, not a reconciled distribution story. Design partners (Series-B devtools; AI lab) and the **10x** agent-CI cost-reduction claim remain subject-only [C8][C9].

Independently archived product presence (not a substitute for cleared scale): GitHub — 412 stars, weekly releases for 20 weeks, founder-dominated commits [C10]; Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win (~400 teams) [C11]; June 2026 launch thread with three self-identified production users [C12]. Forum adjudication (agent-derived) treated deck install/MRR figures as uncleared non-evidence until reconciled, while keeping diligence open on shipping/launch presence rather than killing on registry totals alone [C13].
sections.hypotheses:
# Investment hypotheses — Ferrite

Why underwrite a $100k fast check (or not), with deck commercial magnitudes at zero weight until reconciled. Forum residual tempering confidence: write on execution residue vs wait for billing/telemetry remains open [`agent-derived`].

## Team quality

Solo technical founder Mara Voss (Berlin), first-time, no prior fundraise — cold start in fund scope [C14]. Subject background (ex-infra mid-size fintech; internal remote-cache ~60% CI cut) is uncleared pedigree, not load-bearing [C15].

Independent execution residue: weekly releases for 20 weeks, founder-dominated commit history, real CI coverage, and measurable open-source presence [C16]; infrastructure-track win at Hack-Nation (~400 teams) [C17]; launch-thread technical depth with self-identified production users [C18]. Contested deck installs/MRR are zero-weighted for the founder read [`agent-derived` axes].

## Market wedge

Product thesis: content-addressed build cache that deduplicates agent-generated CI build steps across a fleet [C19]. Structural why-now (LLM-agent CI regenerating steps → dedupe newly valuable vs classic remote cache) is room-held and undisputed as a market inflection; capture size is thin in the record [`agent-derived`]. Competitive/social validation sits in the hackathon win and launch presence [C17][C18], not in cleared TAM.

## Stickiness / retention mechanics

No archived retention, churn, cohort, or reuse-rate evidence on file. Cache hit-rate / fleet network effects are product-logic only — not evidenced. **Gap.**

## Traction signal

**Independent (usable residue):** shipping cadence and OSS footprint [C16]; hackathon [C17]; launch with three self-identified production users [C18].

**Subject commercial claims (uncleared non-evidence until reconciled):** 3,100 active installs / 240 orgs [C20]; $18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months [C21]; two design partners (names withheld) [C22]; 10x agent-CI cost cut (internal benchmark) [C23]. Forum converged: these cannot carry underwriting until reconciled [`agent-derived`].

**Contradiction (do not smooth):** public registry shows 2,900 lifetime downloads and ~340/week — lifetime below claimed active-install count [C24] vs [C20]. Independent presence does not repair the measurement chain; registry alone does not kill diligence [`agent-derived` adjudication].

## Defensibility

No IP, switching-cost study, or moat artifact on file. Near-term defense argument is craft + shipping velocity on a narrow infra wedge [C16][C19] — thin, execution-contingent, not a proven moat. **Gap on durable defensibility.**

## Expansion path

Ask is $500k pre-seed / $100k fast check [C25]. Path beyond agent-CI cache (adjacent CI surfaces, enterprise fleet, multi-runtime) is not in the archived record. Design-partner substance uncleared [C22]. **Gap.**

## Hypothesis (decision-facing)

Under registry-order compression with deck commercial claims at zero weight: the investable residue is verified craft + agent-CI why-now + reachable production users — not deck-order scale. Confidence on that residue is **medium** and forked: write-side seats treat it as enough for $100k; wait-side require reconciled billing/telemetry/channel map first [`agent-derived` residual]. Same-week instrument (user contacts + billing/telemetry + channel map + 10x protocol) collapses the fork; this document does not settle it.
sections.swot:
## Strengths
- Solo technical founder with independently verified shipping velocity: ~1,400 commits over 11 months (86% founder), weekly releases for 20 weeks, real CI test coverage [C26].
- Competitive validation: Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon (May 2026) infrastructure-track winner in a ~400-team field [C27].
- Post-launch presence: aggregator front page (~6h), 214 points / 89 comments; founder answered 41 in-thread; three commenters self-identified as production users [C28].
- Open-source footprint: 412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors [C29].
- Thesis fit (agent-derived axes, not underwriting): cold-start solo technical founder; contested deck installs/MRR zero-weighted for founder read; execution residue held under pressure [C30].

## Weaknesses
- **Live contradiction:** deck claims 3,100 active installations / 240 orgs [C31] vs public registry 2,900 lifetime downloads / ~340/week [C32] — lifetime downloads below claimed active installs.
- Deck $18k MRR and +40% MoM for 3 months are subject-only; no billing artifact on file [C33].
- 10x agent-CI cost-reduction claim is an internal/deck benchmark only [C34].
- Design partners (Series-B devtools; AI lab) named only as withheld deck claims — no confirmation on file [C35].
- Solo-founder concentration: commit share is founder-dominated [C26]; no prior institutional round (cold start) [C36].

## Opportunities
- Structural why-now (agent-derived market axis): LLM-agent CI regenerating build steps across fleets makes content-addressed dedupe newly valuable vs classic remote cache; room did not dispute the inflection, only timing of capture proof [C37].
- Category signal from hackathon win + launch-thread production users suggests early demand for agent-CI cost tooling [C27][C28].
- If distribution beyond the public registry (private mirrors, binaries, containers, self-host) reconciles install counts, contested scale could clear without a product pivot [C32] — channel map not yet on file (see gaps).

## Risks
- If commercial checks compress paid/active scale toward registry order, the strongest deck traction narrative fails and the ask rests on execution residue alone [C31][C32].
- Forum residual (agent-derived, unresolved): under that compression, write $100k on shipping + inflection + reachable users vs wait for reconciled billing/telemetry — stable fork; confidence tempered, not averaged [C38].
- Collapse risk on the instrument path: if same-week user contacts show no production residue, write-side joins wait; if reconciliation fails and residue disappears, all seats wait [C38].
- Prior-employer remote-cache / ~60% CI-cut claim unverified — employment/reference check not on file [C39].
- Market sizing and competitor clusters are thin in the record; public registry-order capture (if sole surface) implies early, not proven, category share [C32][C37].
sections.problem_product:
## Problem

LLM-agent CI regenerates build steps across a fleet of runs. Teams pay repeatedly for agent-generated steps that recur across jobs; that duplicated compute is the cost surface Ferrite says it targets. [C40]

No independent customer spend study or workload taxonomy is on file. Independent sources do confirm the category label: content-addressed caching for agent CI. [C41]

## Product / process

Ferrite is a content-addressed build cache that deduplicates agent-generated build steps across a fleet so matching steps are served from cache instead of re-executed. [C42]

Stated mechanism, as far as the record goes:

1. Agent-driven CI emits build steps that recur across runs and organizations.
2. Steps are addressed by content.
3. Cache hits avoid re-execution → claimed cost reduction on agent-driven CI workloads. [C43]

No architecture diagram, hash scheme, API surface, or CI-provider integration sequence is on file. Process depth stops at the one-liner and category framing.

## What is independently real about the product

A public repository ships on a weekly release cadence (20 weeks), with 1,400 commits over 11 months and CI showing real test coverage. [C44]

May 2026: infrastructure-track win at Hack-Nation (field of ~400 teams) under the same product framing. [C41]

June 2026 launch: three commenters identified themselves as production users in-thread. [C45]

A public package registry distributes the product at 2,900 lifetime downloads and ~340/week — measurable presence, not deck-order install scale. [C46]

## Uncleared product-magnitude claims

The 10x cost-reduction claim is subject-only (application one-liner and deck internal benchmark). No protocol, workload mix, or customer before/after CI spend artifact is on file. [C43]

Design partners (Series-B devtools company; AI lab) are named in the ask materials with names withheld — partnership scope and status unverified. [C47]

Forum adjudication (agent-derived, not company fact): deck-scale commercial magnitudes are uncleared non-evidence; the content-addressed agent-CI product idea was not rejected — measurement debt on the ask, not a pivot thesis. Confidence on product fit stays medium; confidence on product magnitude stays low until the same-week instrument (reachable users + billing/telemetry/channel map + 10x protocol) collapses or confirms. [C48]
sections.traction:
# Traction & KPIs — Ferrite

**Read for the 24h check:** Deck commercial magnitudes are uncleared and in tension with the only independent distribution counter. Independently verified presence is thin (shipping, launch self-ID users, hackathon)—not cleared MRR, installs, or unit economics. Confidence on scale: **low** until billing/telemetry reconcile; confidence that *some* production residue exists: **medium** on launch-thread users alone.

## Customer / install scale

Deck asserts **3,100 active installations across 240 organizations** [C49]. Public package registry shows **2,900 lifetime downloads** and **~340/week** [C50]—lifetime downloads sit *below* the claimed active-install count. Forum adjudication (agent-derived) converged that the deck install/org figures are uncleared non-evidence until reconciled; they do not carry underwriting [C51].

**Tension (do not smooth):** either distribution is multi-channel (private mirrors, binaries, containers, self-host) and the registry undercounts, or the deck’s “active install” definition is inflated/undefined. No channel map or telemetry definition is on file.

Independent presence (not customer count): GitHub **412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors** [C52]; launch thread had **three commenters who identified themselves as production users** [C53]. Design partners (“Series-B devtools”; “AI lab”) are deck-only, names withheld—unverified partnership substance [C54].

## Revenue / ARR / growth

Deck: **$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months** [C55]. No payment-processor export, billing artifact, plan mix, refunds, or customer-count export on file. ARR is not independently supported; treating $18k×12 as ARR would be invention. Forum (agent-derived): treat MRR/MoM as uncleared non-evidence [C51].

## Unit economics

**CAC, sales cycle, churn, LTV, payback:** not disclosed. No pricing, conversion, or retention series on file.

## Usage metrics

**DAU/WAU/MAU, cache hit rates, CI minutes saved, seats per org:** not disclosed. Closest independent usage proxy is registry download velocity [C50] and self-identified production users on launch [C53]—neither is a DAU series.

## Performance claim (cost)

Deck/application: **10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI** (internal benchmark) [C56]. No protocol, workload mix, or customer before/after spend artifact on file.

## What the independent record does support

Sustained shipping (weekly releases ~20 weeks; founder-dominated commit history) [C57] and Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win [C58] are **execution/validation signals**, not cleared commercial traction. They keep diligence open under registry-order compression; they do not substitute for reconciled paid/active scale.

## Diligence that settles this page

Same-week packet implied by forum instrument (agent-derived): (1) contact launch-thread production users or founder intros for unscripted agent-CI win; (2) trailing-4-month billing export + active-install/org telemetry with distribution-channel map + 10x protocol. Until then, primary magnitudes stay contested or blank.
sections.team_history:
## Team & history

### Founder
**Mara Voss** is Ferrite's sole founder: technical, based in Berlin [C59]. She is a first-time founder with no prior fundraise [C60]. Background is self-reported only: four years of infrastructure work at a mid-size fintech, including an internal remote-cache that she says cut CI spend ~60% [C61]. That employment and impact claim is **unverified** on file—no manager/peer reference or employment artifact is attached.

### Execution residue (independent)
What the record can verify about her as an operator is shipping and public presence, not pedigree:

- Public repo history shows sustained, founder-dominated delivery: **1,400 commits over 11 months**, **86% by the founder**, **weekly releases for the last 20 weeks**, plus CI with real test coverage [C62]. The same archive shows **9 external contributors**, **412 stars**, **61 forks**—contributors, not a disclosed exec team [C63].
- May 2026: **Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon** infrastructure-track winner (~400 teams) for Ferrite as content-addressed caching for agent CI [C64].
- 2 June 2026 launch: aggregator front page ~6 hours; founder answered **41** in-thread comments with technical depth; **three** commenters self-identified as production users [C65].

An illustrative Founder Score fixture on file marks `cold_start: true` and scores **71 [58, 80]** (medium confidence), explicitly tying the score to shipping cadence and the hackathon win—not to deck traction [C66]. That fixture is labeled fictional / not Memory-layer output; weigh, do not treat as an independent underwriting authority.

### Exec team & single-founder red flag
No co-founder, CTO/CEO split, or other named executives appear in the application or archived signals [C59]. The operating footprint is Mara plus open-source contributors on the public repo [C63]. Example Fund I's stated posture puts pre-track-record / cold-start technical founders in scope and prioritizes shipping velocity over pedigree; solo founding is therefore a diligence concentration risk, not an automatic disqualifier under that thesis.

Independently archived residue available for solo-founder assessment: weekly shipping, the hackathon win, and launch-thread self-identified production users [C67]. Deck installs/MRR are not part of that independent residue. Agent-derived forum adjudication (deliberation input, not a company signal) left a **stable fork**: write $100k on that residue vs wait for reconciled billing/telemetry. Comfort with the single-founder concentration is therefore **conditional** on the same-week user-contact + billing/telemetry instrument collapsing that fork—not settled by pedigree or by the fixture score.

### Company timeline (founding → today)
Exact legal incorporation / founding date is **not disclosed**. Observable product history as of mid-July 2026:

| When | What | Trust |
|------|------|--------|
| ~11 months of commit history (through mid-July 2026 observation) | Founder-dominated build of the public Ferrite repo | verified-online [C62] |
| May 2026 | Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win | verified-online [C64] |
| 2 June 2026 | Public launch; self-identified production users in-thread | verified-online [C65] |
| 17 July 2026 | Applied for $500k pre-seed / $100k fast check (solo technical founder, no prior fundraise) | subject application [C59][C60] |

No prior institutional round is claimed; none is evidenced on file beyond the cold-start flag [C60][C66].
sections.technology_defensibility:
# Technology & defensibility — Ferrite

## Product wedge (what must stay proprietary)

Ferrite’s stated product is a **content-addressed build cache** that targets **LLM-agent CI**: deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet so those runs cost less [C68]. The architectural primitive—content-addressed caching—is not itself rare in CI/infra; the investable claim is a **narrow adaptation**: agent workloads regenerate similar steps with high churn, so digest-keyed reuse across fleets becomes newly valuable versus yesterday’s remote cache [C69]. That why-now framing was not rejected in deliberation; contested commercial magnitudes were treated as measurement debt on the ask, not a pivot [C70].

**Proprietary vs commoditizable (record-bounded):**

| Layer | Status on record | Defensibility read |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Content-addressed cache / remote-cache pattern | Category pattern; founder claims prior fintech remote-cache work [C71] | **Commoditizable** if Ferrite is only “another remote cache” |
| Agent-CI–aware step identity, fleet dedupe, cost path for agent runs | Product thesis + shipping artifact; no hashing/protocol whitepaper on file | **Candidate proprietary surface**—unverified depth |
| OSS client / public package | Public repo + registry package exist [C72][C73] | Client/distribution **commoditizes**; value must sit in hosted coordination, hit-rate, or closed server |
| Multi-tenant digest corpus / hit-rate learning | Thesis only | **Potential data moat**—no telemetry proving compound hit rates |

Without an architecture artifact, treat “agent-aware identity + fleet coordination” as the **only** non-commodity hypothesis; do not upgrade it to proven IP.

## Architecture & shipping evidence

Independent archive shows a real, actively maintained codebase: 412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors, ~1,400 commits over 11 months (86% founder), weekly releases for 20 weeks, CI with real test coverage [C72]. That is execution footprint on the product, not proof of a locked-in protocol.

Competitive/social validation of the *idea framing* (not of proprietary depth): Hack-Nation infrastructure-track win (~400 teams) for “content-addressed caching for agent CI” [C74]; launch-thread technical Q&A with three self-identified production users [C75]. Production-user presence supports “works for someone,” not “moat compounds.”

**10x agent-CI cost cut** remains subject-only and protocol-less on file [C76]. Forum write-side seats would still underwrite craft + reachable users at this check size; wait-side seats refuse to let presence stand in for a reproducible protocol [C77]. Confidence on technical *advantage* stays capped until a benchmark packet exists.

## Data moat

The compounding story, if real, is **networked cache residue**: more agent fleets → more content-addressed digests → higher cross-run/org hit rates → lower marginal CI cost → stickier default in agent pipelines [C78]. That is **reconstructed thesis**, not observed telemetry.

Scale evidence that would *size* the corpus is contested: deck claims 3,100 active installs / 240 orgs [C79]; public registry shows 2,900 lifetime downloads and ~340/week [C73]. Lifetime downloads below claimed active installs is an open contradiction [C79][C73]. Until distribution surfaces (registry, mirrors, binaries, containers, self-host) are mapped, **moat magnitude is unknown**—and public-registry-order capture, if that is the only surface, implies an early corpus, not a deep one [C80].

Open distribution also bites defensibility: a public package and starred repo lower switching costs on the client path [C72][C73]. Any durable advantage must be argued in **server-side coordination, corpus exclusivity, or integration depth**—none of which are artifact-verified here.

## Why advantage would compound (conditional)

Compounding holds **only if** three conditions clear diligence this document cannot settle:

1. **Agent-step identity is sticky and hard to clone well** (protocol/hashing depth)—gap.
2. **Hit rates improve with shared corpus size** (telemetry)—gap.
3. **Users stay for cost/latency residue**, not for a one-line CLI—same-week user contacts are the forum’s instrument to test production residue [C77][C75].

If those fail, Ferrite collapses toward a competent remote-cache in a hot category: shipping remains real [C72]; defensibility does not.

## Tech diligence that binds this section

Open items owned here (routed, not cleared): distribution-channel map reconciling installs vs registry; reproducible 10x protocol + workload mix; design-partner technical scope; prior-employer remote-cache reference; optional spot-check of release notes/CI only if commercial checks fail. Design-partner names and integration depth are withheld/undisclosed on the record.

**Bottom line for the assembler:** Wedge = agent-CI content-addressed dedupe; pattern = partly commoditizable; moat = **unproven compounding corpus**; load-bearing independent tech evidence = shipping cadence + category validation + thin production-user residue—not deck scale or 10x. Stable fork on whether that residue underwrites $100k does **not** resolve inside this document [C77].
sections.market_sizing:
# Market sizing — Ferrite

## Scope of what the record can support

This document needs top-down and/or bottom-up TAM / SAM / SOM with explicit assumptions. **Dollar TAM / SAM / SOM cannot be grounded from evidence on file** — no industry spend series, buyer counts, verified ARPU, attach rates, or competitor-revenue clusters appear in signals or the application. What follows is (1) a qualitative category/why-now, (2) bottom-up **capture proxies** under stated assumptions, and (3) open tensions that bound how far those proxies can be read as obtainable market. Agent-derived forum market read (bullish / improving / medium confidence; sizing thin) is deliberation material, not an independent size estimate; the residual write-vs-wait fork under registry-order compression keeps capture confidence capped.

## Category definition

Ferrite’s wedge is a content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet [C81]. Fund sector fit (ai-infrastructure / devtools / applied-AI B2B) is thesis-side context, not a measured market.

## Why-now (qualitative; not sized)

The demand hypothesis is that LLM-agent CI regenerates build steps across fleets such that content-addressed dedupe is newly valuable versus classic remote cache [C81]. Independent corroboration in the file is **presence and competitive attention**, not spend:

- Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon (May 2026), infrastructure track winner — “Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI”; judged field of ~400 teams [C82].
- Launch post (2 Jun): front page of a major aggregator ~6 hours; 214 points; 89 comments; three commenters self-identified as production users [C83].
- Public repo: 412 stars, weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks [C84].

These support a live problem/category and some production residue. They do **not** establish TAM, SAM, or paid share.

## Top-down TAM / SAM / SOM

| Layer | Constructed? | Note |
| --- | --- | --- |
| TAM | No | No CI / remote-cache / agent-CI spend figures on file |
| SAM | No | No verified count of orgs running agent CI, willingness-to-pay, or price band |
| SOM | Proxy only | Contested deck commercial claims vs registry-order distribution; see bottom-up |

Any numeric top-down stack would be fabrication and is omitted.

## Bottom-up capture proxies (assumptions stated)

**Assumption set A — if deck commercial claims clear (currently uncleared).** Deck asserts “3,100 active installations across 240 organizations” and “$18k MRR, +40% MoM for 3 months” [C85][C86]. Arithmetic under A: $18k × 12 ≈ **$216k ARR** as current paid footprint; 240 orgs as current org SAM penetration. **Status:** subject-only; no billing export on file; forum diligence treats these as non-carrying until reconciled. Do not use Path A as underwritable SOM.

**Assumption set B — if the public registry is the primary install surface.** Registry: “340 downloads/week, 2,900 downloads total lifetime,” with lifetime downloads below the claimed active-install count [C87]. Under B, near-term install footprint is **registry-order** (early capture), not deck-order. **Status:** verified-online for the public package only; other channels (private mirrors, binaries, containers, self-host) are unmapped — B is a lower-bound hypothesis if those channels exist, not a hard ceiling.

**Tension (open):** deck active-install / org scale [C85] vs registry lifetime downloads [C87]. Independent shipping/launch presence [C82][C83][C84] vs registry-implied distribution scale [C87]. Capture for SOM stays **forked** until a distribution-channel map and same-window telemetry/billing reconcile the definitions.

**Willingness-to-pay bridge (not closed).** Deck/application claim “10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI workloads (internal benchmark)” [C88] and two design partners (Series-B devtools; AI lab; names withheld) [C89] could, if verified with spend artifacts, convert save-rate × CI bill into a SAM price anchor. Protocol, workload mix, and partner confirmation are not on file — no dollars attached.

## Explicit non-results

- No reconstructed industry TAM (would require external spend data absent here).
- No competitor cluster sizing.
- No EU vs US revenue split despite fund geography and Berlin founder location.
- Contested primary magnitudes mean **SOM confidence is low** even where presence signals are high-confidence [C82][C83][C84][C85][C87].
sections.competition:
## Competition

### Record posture
Named competitor products, vendors, and clusters are **absent** from the application and from every independent signal on file (GitHub, registry, hackathon archive, launch thread). This document cannot deliver the judges’ brief of named clusters with per-cluster differentiation; the gaps inventory is the binding statement. What follows is Ferrite’s stated wedge, category-presence signals, contested capture scale, and explicitly reconstructed later-threat *classes* (not evidenced entities).

### Stated wedge (claimed differentiation; no named foil)
Ferrite’s application positions the product as a “Content-addressed build cache that makes LLM-agent CI runs 10x cheaper by deduplicating agent-generated build steps across a fleet” [C90]. Deck claims include “10x cost reduction on agent-driven CI workloads (internal benchmark)” [C91]. Both are subject claims of how Ferrite differs from unspecified alternatives. The record has no named competitor comparison and no independent competitive teardown.

### Category / presence signals (field validation, not a rival map)
Independent signals show public selection and early production interest without identifying adjacent products:
- Hack-Nation Global AI Hackathon May 2026: winner, infrastructure track, for “Ferrite: content-addressed caching for agent CI”; judged field of ~400 teams [C92].
- GitHub (archived): 412 stars, 61 forks, 9 external contributors; weekly release cadence for the last 20 weeks [C93].
- Launch post, 2 June: front page of a major aggregator for ~6 hours; three commenters identified themselves as production users [C94].

These support category interest and product presence, not a competitive set.

### Capture scale (early vs. locked — contested)
Public package registry: 340 downloads/week, 2,900 downloads total lifetime [C95]. Deck: “3,100 active installations across 240 organizations” [C96]. Lifetime registry downloads sit below the claimed active-install count—tension on file, not smoothed. If verified distribution compresses toward registry order, capture is early rather than category-owning against whoever later ships agent-aware caching.

### Who could become a threat later
**No named later threats are on file.** Reconstructed placeholder classes only (not entities on file): remote-cache / CI platforms adding agent-step dedupe; agent-orchestration vendors bundling cache; a design partner or AI lab shipping an internal equivalent. Design partners appear only as “one Series-B devtools company, one AI lab (names withheld in deck)” [C97]—potential build-vs-buy actors, not competitive intel.

### Confidence
Competition confidence is **low**. Wedge and category presence have anchors; named clusters, per-cluster differentiation, and named later threats cannot be filled without fabrication. Agent-derived forum residual (write on shipping/inflection/reachable users vs wait for billing/telemetry) tempers check timing, not the competitive map—see gaps.

Archived hackathon record (sig-003): infrastructure-track winner, May 2026, ~400-team field, framed as content-addressed caching for agent CI.
Archived launch post (sig-005): aggregator front page ~6h, 214 points / 89 comments; founder answered 41; three commenters self-identified as production users — identities not yet contacted.
Verified public package registry (sig-004): ~340 downloads/week, 2,900 lifetime — lifetime below deck’s 3,100 active-install claim; contradiction recorded, not reconciled.
Deck (sig-001) asserts 3,100 active installs / 240 orgs and $18k MRR +40% MoM for 3 months; forum treated as uncleared non-evidence until billing/telemetry reconcile.

conditions: Same-week: contact the three launch-thread production users or two founder-introduced current users for an unscripted agent-CI cost/latency win.

Same-week: obtain a one-page 10x benchmark protocol (workload mix; any customer before/after CI spend artifact).
Collapse rule — if users evaporate or show no production residue → write-side joins wait (no check).
Collapse rule — if billing/telemetry clears coherent paid/active reality (or an honest correction with residual usage) → wait-side reassesses off the block.
Collapse rule — if reconciliation fails and residue disappears → all seats wait (no check).
decision.rationale: Agent-derived recommendation into a human gate — Example Fund I decides within 24h; this memo does not settle the case. Thesis fit is strong (ai-infra/devtools, EU pre-seed, cold-start solo technical founder in scope; shipping velocity over pedigree). Independently verified residue is craft + agent-CI why-now + thin reachable production users (weekly shipping 20 weeks, Hack-Nation win, launch-thread self-ID users); deck 3,100 installs / $18k MRR / +40% MoM are uncleared non-evidence and contradict public-registry lifetime downloads. Forum adjudication left a stable fork, not a blended score: write $100k on that execution residual vs wait for reconciled billing/telemetry before any check. Recommendation is contested pending the same-week instrument above — do not fund on deck scale, do not pass solely on registry totals.
